questions.question: What is the correct order for link pseudo-classes?
questions.explanation: LVHA order: :link → :visited → :hover → :active. Memory trick: LoVe HAte.

questions.question: Why should you never remove focus outlines without providing alternatives?
options: It makes the site unusable for keyboard users
questions.explanation: Removing focus outlines prevents keyboard users from seeing which element is focused, making navigation impossible.

questions.question: Which pseudo-class applies styles to links being clicked?
questions.explanation: :active applies during the moment a link is being clicked (between mousedown and mouseup).

questions.question: What does a:link select?
questions.explanation: a:link selects anchor elements that have an href attribute and have not been visited by the user.

questions.question: What is the default text-decoration for links?
questions.explanation: Browsers apply text-decoration: underline to links by default to distinguish them from regular text.

questions.question: How do you change the cursor when hovering over a link?
options: Use the cursor property on the link
Links automatically show a pointer cursor
questions.explanation: Links automatically show a pointer cursor (hand icon) — this is default browser behavior, not a CSS setting.

questions.question: What does a:visited do?
options: Styles links that have been clicked on by the user
Styles links that are currently being visited
Styles all links regardless of visit state
questions.explanation: a:visited applies styles to links whose href matches pages in the browser's history.

questions.question: For accessibility, what should indicate a link besides color?
questions.explanation: Color alone shouldn't distinguish links from regular text. Underline or other non-color cues help color-blind users.

questions.question: How do you remove the underline from a link?
questions.explanation: text-decoration: none removes the underline from links, but consider adding hover underlining for clarity.

questions.question: What is the difference between :focus and :focus-visible?
options: :focus applies always; :focus-visible applies only for keyboard focus
questions.explanation: :focus fires on any focus. :focus-visible only shows the focus ring for keyboard navigation, not mouse clicks.

questions.question: How can you style a link that opens in a new tab differently?
questions.explanation: The attribute selector a[target="_blank"] targets links that open in new tabs for custom styling.

questions.question: What does styling a:link with color: inherit do?
options: Makes the link use the parent element's text color
questions.explanation: color: inherit makes the link use its parent element's text color, useful for inline links in paragraphs.

questions.question: What is a link button pattern?
options: A link that looks like a button using CSS padding, background, and borders
A button that navigates like a link
A link inside a button element
A button that uses the <a> tag
questions.explanation: Link buttons style anchor elements with button-like padding, background, border-radius, and hover states.

questions.question: Which pseudo-class would you use to style a link when a user hovers over it?
questions.explanation: :hover applies styles when the user's pointer is over the element.

questions.question: How do you indicate the current page in a navigation menu?
options: Use a class like .active or .current on the current page's link
Browsers automatically style the current page
questions.explanation: Add an active/current class to the navigation item corresponding to the current page, then style it with CSS.

questions.question: What is the minimum recommended tap target size for links on mobile?
questions.explanation: WCAG recommends interactive targets be at least 44x44px for comfortable touch interaction.

questions.question: How do you create an animated underline effect on hover?
options: Use transition on border-bottom or a pseudo-element
questions.explanation: Animated underlines can be created by transitioning border-bottom width or using a pseudo-element with scaleX animation.

questions.question: What does a[href^="https://"] select?
options: Links with https in any part of the URL
Links starting with https:// — useful for styling external links
questions.explanation: The ^= attribute selector matches links where the href value starts with the specified string.

questions.question: How do links usually appear by default in most browsers?
options: Blue underlined for unvisited, purple underlined for visited
Black bold for unvisited, gray for visited
Blue with no underline for unvisited, purple with underline for visited
All links are blue with no distinction
questions.explanation: Default browser styles: unvisited links are blue and underlined; visited links are purple and underlined.

questions.question: What does the :any-link pseudo-class do?
options: Matches all links regardless of visit state
Matches links that are not yet visited
Matches links with any href value
questions.explanation: :any-link matches any element with an href attribute, combining both :link and :visited into one selector.
